title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Colts applied a transition tag to QB Daniel Jones, allowing Alec Pierce to potentially enter free agency.
Alec Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch for two consecutive seasons, making him a top free-agent option.
Patriots may need to restructure Stefon Diggs' contract to afford Alec Pierce without sacrificing draft capital.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, a Vermont postal worker, is accused of discarding mail, facing petty larceny and unlawful mischief charges.
Police found opened and unopened mail, including holiday cards with money, in a North Troy dumpster and her vehicle.
Morisseau has been suspended from USPS; the case has been referred to the USPS Inspector General for further investigation.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An 80-year-old man and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street in Foxborough.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville firefighters extinguished the fire within an hour despite major damage to the home.
The cause of the early Tuesday morning fire is under investigation by local authorities.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston's Estella's restaurant may lose its liquor license after hosting a party with Patriots players and violating regulations.
During the party, police found hookahs, strippers, and alcohol brought in by the players after hours.
Owner Helder George Brandao claims Patriots players brought their own items and left without paying the tab.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest national AP exam scores, with 35.8% scoring 3 or higher in 2025.
Massachusetts ranked first overall and had the highest percentage of Black or African American students taking AP exams.
The state subsidized over 90,783 AP exams, promoting access for nearly 50,000 low-income students.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Council members are collecting a 50% pay raise that they initially campaigned to repeal.
New councilors cited a lack of public input as a reason for running, intending to address the raise process.
Several councilors plan to donate their raises to nonprofits or charitable causes due to legal constraints on repeal.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox remain interested in trading for Astros' Isaac Paredes, potentially involving Jarren Duran, despite recent infield acquisitions.
Paredes, a power hitter, hit 20 home runs in 102 games last season and could benefit from Fenway Park's dimensions.
Acquiring Paredes could resolve Red Sox's outfield logjam while providing more DH opportunities for players like Masataka Yoshida.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN mock draft predicts Patriots will select Texas A&M edge rusher Cashius Howell with 31st overall pick.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen in 2025.
Patriots seek to bolster pass rush with Howell, despite questions about his ability as a three-down player.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was convicted for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel in Essex County Superior Court.
Claire Gravel, a 20-year-old student, was strangled to death on June 30, 1986, and her body was found in Beverly.
A new lead in 2012 and DNA evidence on Gravel's clothing helped convict Carey after his 2022 indictment.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, of Weare, N.H., accused of shooting at a Black man who mistakenly drove to her property.
Durgin faces up to $5,000 fines per violation of New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act and several criminal charges.
Prosecutors claim Durgin threatened the man with a gun, fired shots, and made racially motivated comments.
